vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1574: Codex as a first-class harness (pipeline peer to Claude Code and Pi)

plan.title: Codex as a first-class harness (pipeline peer to Claude Code and Pi)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Codex exists in the repo only as an OpenAI auth provider (CLIProxy) and a cosmetic skill-sync target. There is no `codex` runtime: `src/lib/runtimes/` is claude-code + pi only, and both `RuntimeName` and `Harness` are `'claude-code' | 'pi'`. Picking a GPT model routes through Pi or Claude Code — the `codex` binary is never spawned as a Panopticon agent. The pipeline cannot spawn, message, health-check, cost-track, resume, or recover a Codex agent. Critically, getHarness() (contracts/types.ts) normalizes any unknown runtime string to 'claude-code', so every missed touch-point silently degrades a Codex agent to Claude Code rather than erroring — completeness of the touch-point map is the correctness contract.
narratives.Proposal: Add Codex as the third first-class harness by implementing the AgentRuntime abstraction for it, modeled method-by-method on Pi (src/lib/runtimes/pi.ts). Transport: Phase-1 `codex exec resume <thread-id> <prompt>` (stable surface); the experimental `codex app-server` daemon is a deferred phase-2 follow-up. Context via a per-agent CODEX_HOME/AGENTS.md (sidesteps the project-AGENTS.md three-writer collision). Heartbeat/done via a per-agent CODEX_HOME config.toml `notify` hook. Cost via parsing the rollout JSONL `token_count` records. Sessions resolved by captured thread-id → rollout file (Claude-style external store, not Pi's per-agent dir). Widen every harness/runtime union and every reject-unknown-harness validator, add an explicit harness-policy arm so Codex does not inherit Pi's Anthropic-subscription ToS block, and surface Codex in every picker. Claude Code stays the default; Codex is opt-in per spawn. All work ships as one complete feature.

autoDecisions.summary: Decompose into 24 beads across type-widening, runtime adapter, launcher, cost/session parsing, context layering, conversations/memory, pickers/doctor, and an end-to-end pipeline verify — following the PRD's build-order phases but splitting each phase into independently-reviewable beads.
autoDecisions.rationale: PRD §8 phases are build order, not bead boundaries. The planning prompt mandates aggressive decomposition into independently reviewable/mergeable units.

autoDecisions.summary: Foundation bead `types-widen` bundles ALL compile-enforced sites (union members + every Record<Harness>/Schema.Literals that fails to compile when codex is added) plus the start-harness.test.ts fixture fix, because they are the minimal atomic change that keeps `npm run typecheck` green. Runtime-`if` consumer branches that fail SILENT (not at typecheck) are split into their own additive beads.
autoDecisions.rationale: Adding a union member breaks Record<Harness> exhaustiveness (HARNESS_MARKERS, HARNESS_DEFAULT_MODEL) and ContextHarnessPreviews Schema decode at compile time, and flips start-harness.test.ts:107 (which asserts 'codex' is invalid) red. These cannot land separately without a red tree. Additive runtime checks (validators, pickers, policy) compile fine without codex, so they are independent beads.

autoDecisions.summary: Phase-1 delivery transport is `codex exec resume`; the `codex app-server` unix-socket daemon is explicitly out of scope for this issue (deferred phase-2 follow-up).
autoDecisions.rationale: PRD D-2: exec-resume is on Codex's stable CLI surface; app-server is tagged [experimental] and its bindings must be regenerated per upgrade. Shipping on the stable surface first is the lower-risk v1; the daemon is a fast-follow.

autoDecisions.summary: Codex harness context is delivered via a per-agent CODEX_HOME/AGENTS.md, NOT the project-root AGENTS.md.
autoDecisions.rationale: PRD §7.2 / D-4: three writers (Pi context, Codex context, skill mirror) would otherwise contend for the same managed region of the project AGENTS.md. Per-agent CODEX_HOME (option c) sidesteps the collision entirely and is consistent with the per-agent notify/heartbeat design (D-5).

autoDecisions.summary: Only 4 beads carry requiresInspection:true (types-widen, runtime-skeleton, spawn-agent, context-layering).
autoDecisions.rationale: These are the genuine foundations: the cross-cutting type contract everything imports, the adapter shape every runtime method builds on, the per-agent CODEX_HOME spawn contract heartbeat/context/delivery depend on, and the AGENTS.md ownership decision (§7.2). The umbrella's size justifies >3; the remaining 20 beads are mechanical/leaf/test work whose defects surface at typecheck, lint, the verification gate, or end-of-MR review.

items.title: Widen canonical harness/runtime unions and all compile-enforced records/schemas to include 'codex'

narrative.Action: Add 'codex' to every canonical type union AND every compile-enforced Record<Harness>/Schema site so `npm run typecheck` stays green across all packages, with safe stub/default values. Unions: RuntimeName (runtimes/types.ts:20), Harness + KNOWN_HARNESSES (contracts/types.ts:49,51), FlywheelHarness (contracts/flywheel.ts:9), ArtifactAgentHarness (contracts/artifacts.ts:15), LauncherHarness (launcher-generator.ts:7), HarnessMarker + HARNESS_MARKERS + KNOWN_HARNESS_MARKERS (context-layers/harness.ts:27-36), CONTEXT_PREVIEW_HARNESSES + ContextHarnessPreviews struct codex:Schema.String (contracts/context-layers.ts:5,138-143). Compile-enforced records: HARNESS_DEFAULT_MODEL (chat ModelPicker.tsx:30) and the ModelPicker Harness type (ModelPicker.tsx:14). Also fix the now-red fixture in start-harness.test.ts:107 (swap the 'codex'-as-invalid case to 'cursor'). This bead introduces no runtime behavior — codex is a known-but-inert harness afterward.

subItems.title: RuntimeName, Harness, KNOWN_HARNESSES, FlywheelHarness, ArtifactAgentHarness, LauncherHarness all include 'codex'
HARNESS_MARKERS / HarnessMarker / KNOWN_HARNESS_MARKERS include a codex marker; CONTEXT_PREVIEW_HARNESSES and ContextHarnessPreviews struct include codex (Schema decode succeeds)
Frontend HARNESS_DEFAULT_MODEL Record and ModelPicker Harness type include codex with a defensible default model
start-harness.test.ts no longer uses 'codex' as the invalid-harness fixture; `npm run typecheck` is green in root, contracts, and frontend

items.title: Surface Codex in all frontend harness pickers and literal mirrors

narrative.Action: Add Codex to the data-driven and hardcoded picker sites so it is selectable everywhere Pi is: ModelPicker HARNESS_OPTIONS ({id:'codex',label:'Codex'}) and pickModelForHarness default branch (shared/ModelPicker/ModelPicker.tsx:17-20,412); chat ModelPicker loadStoredHarness + harness badge (ModelPicker.tsx:170-176,351); frontend types.ts:86; useRestartAgent.ts:14; useSwitchModel.ts:13; PlanDialog.tsx:63,137,200; RolesPanel.tsx:9 AND the hardcoded Flywheel harness <select> <option>s at RolesPanel.tsx:520-521 (NOT data-driven); FlywheelConversationPane.tsx:38,54; useConversationFeed.ts:16,77-80 (mapHarnessToAgent); ForkModal.tsx:156-173 source-authoring guard.

subItems.title: Codex appears as a selectable option in every harness picker including the hardcoded Flywheel RolesPanel select
mapHarnessToAgent and the ForkModal source-authoring guard handle 'codex' without falling through to a wrong default

items.title: Accept 'codex' in every reject-unknown-harness validator and CLI guard

narrative.Action: Widen the runtime validators that currently reject anything not in {claude-code, pi}: config-yaml.ts:1481-1482 (RoleConfig.harness validator) and the role-harness type/default at :302,365,501,639; settings-api.ts:380-381; start.ts:701-703; handoff.ts:25; artifacts.ts:389; artifacts/lifecycle.ts:210; setup/hooks.ts:298; context-layers.ts:160-164. Update the 'must be claude-code or pi' error messages to include codex.

subItems.title: A role configured with harness: codex passes config-yaml and settings-api validation; error messages list codex as valid
All CLI guards (start, handoff, artifacts, lifecycle, context-layers) accept --harness codex

items.title: Add explicit Codex arm to harness-policy so it does not inherit Pi's Anthropic-subscription block

narrative.Action: In harness-policy.ts:69-93, canUseHarnessSync returns ALLOWED for claude-code then the implicit else (commented // harness === 'pi') applies the Pi-Anthropic-subscription ToS block to ANYTHING non-claude — so Codex would wrongly inherit it. Add an explicit `if (harness === 'codex')` arm (ALLOWED; Codex+ChatGPT-subscription is fine, it is not the Pi+Anthropic+subscription case) BEFORE the Pi block, and convert the implicit else into an explicit `=== 'pi'` check. The existing gpt-5.5+api-key block in canUseModelWithAuthSync already applies to all harnesses and is correct for codex — do not change it. Add a `codex:` key to the policy decisions map in routes/settings.ts:829-830.

subItems.title: canUseHarnessSync('codex', …) returns ALLOWED and never applies the Pi-Anthropic-subscription ToS block
The non-claude else branch is now an explicit harness === 'pi' check; a unit test covers the codex arm
routes/settings.ts policy map returns a codex decision cell

items.title: Create src/lib/runtimes/codex.ts skeleton (introspection methods + Effect wrapper) and register it

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/runtimes/codex.ts implementing AgentRuntimeSync and the Effect AgentRuntime, modeled on pi.ts. In this bead implement the introspection methods and the D-7 session model: name ('codex'); getSessionPath resolving $CODEX_HOME/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*-<threadId>.jsonl by a captured thread-id (Claude-style EXTERNAL store — NOT Pi's <agentDir>/sessions); getLastActivity (rollout mtime); listSessions (walk $CODEX_HOME/sessions); isRunning (sessionExists). Stub spawnAgent/sendMessage/killAgent/getHeartbeat/getTokenUsage/getSessionCost as not-yet-implemented (throw or passive default) so the tree compiles. Copy Pi's Effect wrapper shape (pi.ts:406-481) verbatim — hold an inner sync runtime and lift async methods via Effect.tryPromise. Register in runtimes/index.ts: export createCodexRuntimeSync/createCodexRuntime, add to getGlobalRegistry (:93-102), add a harness === 'codex' arm to getRuntimeForAgent (:74-78). This bead establishes the adapter shape and the thread-id-keyed external-session-store model every later runtime method depends on.

subItems.title: codex.ts compiles implementing both AgentRuntimeSync and the Effect AgentRuntime; introspection methods (name, getSessionPath, getLastActivity, listSessions, isRunning) are real
getSessionPath resolves a rollout JSONL by captured thread-id under $CODEX_HOME/sessions (external store, D-7), with a unit test against a fixture rollout tree
getRuntimeForAgent('codex'-harness agent) returns the Codex runtime; the global registry includes it

items.title: Add buildCodexCommand and Codex routing/config fields to launcher-generator

narrative.Action: In launcher-generator.ts add codexMode/codexHome/codexSessionDir to LauncherConfig; route harness === 'codex' in buildCommand/buildNonConversationCommand and the conversation path (:373,458,372) to a new buildCodexCommand modeled on buildPiCommand (:548-613). The work-agent (exec) form emits `codex exec [--json] [-m <model>] -c approval_policy=never -s <sandbox> --skip-git-repo-check` (note the VERIFIED flag traps: exec rejects -a/--ask-for-approval; set approval via -c approval_policy=never). The conversation form emits bare `codex` (TUI). Add harness === 'codex' to the PTY-supervisor early-return (:179) — Codex gets NO PTY supervisor (like Pi).

subItems.title: buildCodexCommand emits a valid headless `codex exec` line with approval_policy=never and a sandbox mode; bare `codex` for TUI/conversation mode
Codex is excluded from PTY-supervisor wrapping; a unit test asserts the generated command for both modes

items.title: Thread the Codex harness through agents.ts (launcher fields, base-command, process-name, harness resolution)

narrative.Action: Thread codex through agents.ts (the heaviest file, ~30 sites). Add getCodexLauncherFields (resolves per-agent CODEX_HOME path + model, returns the codexMode/codexHome/codexSessionDir launcher fields); short-circuit getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand for codex (:347-358, :434-446); spread the codex launcher fields at the three launcher call sites (:2296-2298, :2605-2607, :3606-3618); add 'codex' to the process-name set (:134-135); accept codex in resolveEffectiveHarness (:300); confirm the claude-only guards (:1445, :1547) take the correct codex path. Widen any local harness annotations on AgentState/SpawnOptions/SpawnRunOptions that are not already the contracts Harness type. This bead is mechanical threading; spawnAgent's file-writing contract is a separate bead.

subItems.title: getCodexLauncherFields returns a per-agent CODEX_HOME + model and is spread at all three launcher call sites
getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand and resolveEffectiveHarness handle codex; the process-name set includes codex; typecheck green

items.title: Implement codex.ts spawnAgent: per-agent CODEX_HOME layout, config.toml + AGENTS.md, launch, readiness wait

narrative.Action: Implement spawnAgent in codex.ts modeled on pi.ts:289-357. Create the per-agent CODEX_HOME directory and write its config.toml (with the Panopticon-owned `notify` array pointing at the heartbeat shim — wired fully in notify-heartbeat) and a placeholder AGENTS.md (populated by context-layering). Generate the launcher command (buildCodexCommand), start the tmux session on the panopticon socket (sendKeysAsync only), and detect readiness by the FIRST appearance of the rollout JSONL or the first --json `thread.started` event (D-5) — capture the UUIDv7 thread-id at this point for getSessionPath/sendMessage. This bead crystallizes the per-agent CODEX_HOME directory layout (config.toml + AGENTS.md + sessions) that heartbeat, context delivery, and exec-resume delivery all assume.

subItems.title: spawnAgent creates a per-agent CODEX_HOME with config.toml and AGENTS.md, launches the codex agent in a tmux session, and returns after readiness
The thread-id is captured at spawn from the rollout file / --json thread.started and persisted so getSessionPath and sendMessage can resolve it
A Codex work agent can be spawned in a real workspace and reaches a ready state visible to the orchestrator

items.title: Implement Codex message delivery via `codex exec resume` (D-2 phase 1) and the agents.ts delivery split

narrative.Action: Implement sendMessage in codex.ts as a one-shot `codex exec resume <threadId> <prompt>` per orchestrator message (VERIFIED trap: exec resume rejects -s; set sandbox via -c sandbox_mode=read-only). Add the agents.ts delivery branch: shouldDeliverPromptViaCodex + a writeCodexAgentPrompt analog and the delivery split at :2568-2569 and :2716-2744. Prompt may be passed via stdin (`-`). This is D-2 phase 1 only — app-server is out of scope.

subItems.title: sendMessage delivers a follow-up turn to a running Codex agent via `codex exec resume <threadId>` and the agent processes it (history replayed)
agents.ts routes Codex agent prompts through the codex delivery branch, not the Pi FIFO or Claude PTY path

items.title: Implement codex.ts killAgent with a graceful->SIGKILL ladder that preserves the rollout JSONL

narrative.Action: Implement killAgent in codex.ts modeled on pi.ts:246-287: graceful turn-interrupt/SIGTERM then SIGKILL ladder, killing the tmux session. NEVER delete the rollout JSONL (JSONL-is-sacred; Pi's killAgent preserves session files — mirror exactly).

subItems.title: killAgent stops the Codex agent's tmux session via the SIGTERM->SIGKILL ladder
The rollout JSONL under $CODEX_HOME/sessions is preserved after kill (asserted by test)

items.title: Add codex-parser.ts (parseCodexSessionSync) and wire Codex token/cost into the runtime

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/cost-parsers/codex-parser.ts exporting parseCodexSessionSync that reads the rollout JSONL `event_msg/token_count` records (info.{total_token_usage,last_token_usage}: input/cached_input/output/reasoning_output/total) and returns the shared SessionUsage shape (jsonl-parser.ts:44-55). Wire it into codex.ts getTokenUsage/getSessionCost. cost.ts itself is provider/model-keyed and needs no harness branch — only add codex model pricing rows to DEFAULT_PRICING IF codex routes to un-priced models (check providers.ts; likely gpt-5.x already priced).

subItems.title: parseCodexSessionSync parses token_count records from a fixture rollout into SessionUsage
codex.ts getTokenUsage/getSessionCost return live token + cost for a Codex session; any un-priced codex model has a DEFAULT_PRICING row

items.title: Add the notify shim, config.toml notify wiring, and getHeartbeat/done via agent-turn-complete (D-5)

narrative.Action: Create a small Panopticon `notify` shim script that, given Codex's agent-turn-complete JSON argv (type, thread-id, turn-id, cwd, input-messages, last-assistant-message), writes ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/heartbeat.json and a completed marker. Wire the per-agent CODEX_HOME/config.toml `notify` array (written in spawn-agent) to point at this shim. Implement codex.ts getHeartbeat as the 3-tier ladder (tier1 = notify-written heartbeat.json <60s; tier2 = rollout mtime; tier3 = tmux), matching pi.ts:141-197. Readiness (first rollout / thread.started) is in spawn-agent; this bead adds the turn-end heartbeat + done signal.

subItems.title: On agent-turn-complete the notify shim writes heartbeat.json and a completed marker for the agent
codex.ts getHeartbeat returns a fresh heartbeat after a turn completes (3-tier ladder)
The notify is set via per-agent CODEX_HOME only — the user's global ~/.codex/config.toml is never modified

items.title: Extend session-format-converter for the Codex rollout format (third format)

narrative.Action: In session-format-converter.ts add extractCodexTranscript(raw) for the rollout response_item/event_msg shape; replace the binary extract ternary at :154-155 ('anything not pi -> claude') with a switch(fromHarness) so codex does not fall into the Claude extractor; add a toHarness === 'codex' write branch at :159 seeding a codex rollout + resume pointer. Mind the two-schema trap: rollout-file event names (task_started/agent_message/token_count) differ from the --json stdout names.

subItems.title: extractCodexTranscript parses a fixture rollout JSONL into the shared transcript shape
Conversion from/to codex no longer mis-routes through the Claude extractor; round-trip test passes

items.title: Deliver layered Codex context via per-agent CODEX_HOME/AGENTS.md, avoiding the project-AGENTS.md collision

narrative.Action: Wire Codex into the layered context system (sync.ts, multi-harness since PAN-1566). Add a renderGlobalLayer('codex', …) write that targets the per-agent CODEX_HOME/AGENTS.md (D-4), NOT the user's global ~/.codex/AGENTS.md and NOT the project-root AGENTS.md. Add {{#harness:codex}} block support (the markers are added in types-widen). Resolve the §7.2 three-writer collision via option (c): Codex harness context lives in the per-agent CODEX_HOME/AGENTS.md so it never contends with Pi's managed region or the skill mirror in the project AGENTS.md. Honor the PAN-1569 preservation contract — pre-existing human AGENTS.md content is preserved (managed BEGIN/END region only). This bead encodes the AGENTS.md ownership decision other context beads build on.

subItems.title: renderGlobalLayer('codex') writes Panopticon's layered context to the per-agent CODEX_HOME/AGENTS.md
The project-root AGENTS.md is NOT contended by Codex; Pi's managed region and the skill-mirror blocks are unaffected (§7.2 option c)
Pre-existing human AGENTS.md content is preserved (PAN-1569); {{#harness:codex}} blocks render

items.title: Add a Codex column to the /context preview route and sync targets

narrative.Action: Wire the /context preview for codex in routes/context.ts: PREVIEW_HARNESSES (:68), buildSyncTargets pi->AGENTS.md mapping extended for codex per §7.2 (:259-276), the ?'Claude Code':'Pi' title ternary (:390), the fullPrompt sections (:398-417), and the previews return map (:466-476). routes/hooks.ts:433 if applicable. (The contracts CONTEXT_PREVIEW_HARNESSES + ContextHarnessPreviews codex field were added in types-widen.)

subItems.title: GET /context returns a codex preview column with the correct title and full prompt
Schema decode of the context previews response succeeds with the codex key populated

items.title: Thread Codex through the workspace context bundle and global system-prompt files

narrative.Action: assembleWorkspaceContext already takes a harness; thread codex through workspace.ts:573 (currently hardcodes 'claude-code'). Add a harness === 'codex' arm to claudeSystemPromptFiles (agents.ts:110) so a Codex agent receives its global context file (Codex will not auto-read ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md; it reads the per-agent CODEX_HOME/AGENTS.md).

subItems.title: assembleWorkspaceContext produces a Codex-targeted bundle when harness is codex (no hardcoded claude-code)
claudeSystemPromptFiles returns the correct global context file path for codex

narrative.Action: routes/conversations.ts has many `?? 'claude-code'` defaults and `=== 'pi'`/`=== 'claude-code'` branches governing delivery, session-id capture, and fork rules (:176,181-183,506,710,744,803-826,1015-1089,1894,2077,2130-2233,2888-2920,3163) — add codex arms (delivery via exec-resume from send-message; session-id = captured thread-id; fork rules). In services/conversation-lifecycle.ts the `harness === 'pi'` skip of the ~/.claude/projects scan (:55,187-193,265) must also skip codex (Codex stores under $CODEX_HOME, not ~/.claude/projects).

narrative.Action: Add a codex transcript source in memory/transcript-source.ts (:55,75,109,117,135,176,267) reading the rollout JSONL under $CODEX_HOME/sessions, and thread codex through memory/pipeline.ts:289 and memory/reconciliation.ts:128-129 so Codex agent transcripts feed the memory pipeline like Pi/Claude.

items.title: Ensure Codex agents emit runtime:'codex' in event reducers, snapshots, and handoff defaults

items.title: Thread Codex through the remaining spawn entry points and option/display types

narrative.Action: Pass codex through the remaining spawn entry points (Appendix A.8): planning/spawn-planning-session.ts:564,576; cloister/specialists.ts:141-156; cloister/inspect-agent.ts:183; runtime/claude.ts:106; remote/remote-agents.ts:180; routes/issues.ts:820-822,1028; routes/agents.ts:2128,3129-3136; services/flywheel-actions.ts:40,125,144; cli/commands/flywheel.ts:87,258,274; and the CLI option/display types in plan.ts:8, start.ts:90, strike.ts:16, status.ts:191, workspace.ts:573.

items.title: Add a Codex binary check to pan doctor

narrative.Action: Add checkCodex() to doctor.ts:41-110: detect the `codex` binary on PATH and report `codex --version`, with a remediation hint if missing, modeled on the existing claude/pi checks.

narrative.Action: Extend the tests that enumerate/assert the harness set (Appendix A.16, excluding start-harness.test.ts which is fixed in types-widen): contracts flywheel/artifacts/context-layers tests; tests/lib/context-layers/{harness,render}; settings-api; dashboard context-routes; conversation-lifecycle; memory {transcript-source,reconciliation,pipeline}; artifacts-lifecycle/index-store; cli flywheel.test; integration agent-spawning; work-agent-lifecycle; frontend HomePage/FlywheelPage/issueActions.parity/FlywheelConversationPane. items.title: Add Codex arms to the agents.ts recovery and second-spawn paths

narrative.Action: Add codex handling to the agents.ts recovery/resume paths and the second spawn entry (Appendix A.7): :2527-2530, :2834-2837, :3688, :3876, :4030, :4144-4230. A recovered Codex agent must resume via its captured thread-id (exec-resume) and re-establish its CODEX_HOME, not degrade to claude-code.

items.title: End-to-end: a Codex work agent completes a trivial benchmark issue plan->work->review->test->ship

narrative.Action: Integration verification (PRD §8 phase 5 / §10 success criteria): run a trivial benchmark issue end-to-end on the Codex harness and confirm the full pipeline works — Codex selectable in every picker, spawn + exec-resume delivery + kill, live cost/tokens and health in the dashboard, layered AGENTS.md delivered with human content preserved, resume/recovery, and zero regression to Claude Code / Pi default paths (full suite green). This bead is the feature's acceptance gate, not new surface area.

subItems.title: A trivial benchmark issue completes plan->work->review->test->ship on the Codex harness with visible health, cost, resume, and recovery
Claude Code and Pi default paths are unchanged — full regression suite (typecheck, lint, test root+frontend) is green
Codex is gated by harness-policy in every picker and never inherits Pi's Anthropic-subscription block
